content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes a lowercase English alphabet character as input and uses a 'switch' statement to determine if it's a **Vowel** or **Consonant**.
constraints: Each character is a lowercase English alphabet letter
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single lowercase English alphabet character.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "Vowel" if the character is a vowel (a, e, i, o, u) or "Consonant" otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The single test case contains the character 'i', which is a vowel, so the program prints "Vowel".
The character 't' is not a vowel, therefore the output is "Consonant".
The character 'a' belongs to the vowel set, so the program prints "Vowel".

solution.approach: The problem only requires checking whether a given character belongs to the set of vowels {a, e, i, o, u}. For each test case we read the character and use a switch statement (or a set lookup) to decide the output. This runs in O(1) per character, so the total complexity is O(T). No extra memory is needed beyond a few variables.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case
